evidence: The paper explicitly focuses on "permutation variable importance measure (VIMP)" and compares four permutation-based methods (shadowVIMP, Boruta, rfvimptest, Vita), not impurity-based importance. The biases they address are specific to permutation importance: correlation-induced bias and categorical variable cardinality bias. The paper uses importance for hypothesis testing (Is variable informative? Yes/No) rather than magnitude ranking, testing against null distributions. Since the paper does not use Gini/MDI/variance impurity importance, the impurity-importance bias does not apply to this work.
